eventMetadata.context_description: Post-2026 NFL Draft, trader consensus reflects intense AFC parity, with Ravens leading at 14% implied probability ahead of Bills (12.5%) and Chiefs (10.5%) amid roster upgrades across contenders. Baltimore bolstered its defensive line and added receiver depth to support Lamar Jackson, while Buffalo enhanced its offensive line for Josh Allen's mobility, and Kansas City injected youth into the running back room and edge rush to complement Patrick Mahomes. Rising Texans (9.6%) and Chargers (8.5%) gained from C.J. Stroud's growth and Jim Harbaugh's scheme fitting Justin Herbert, alongside Patriots' quarterback Drake Maye entering Year 2. Cheap rookie contracts and draft hauls keep paths open for playoffs and deep runs, preventing any dominance in this quarterback-rich conference.

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the third-most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent State Duma polls from April 2026 reflect trader uncertainty in the tight contest for third place behind United Russia's projected dominance, with New People (NL) at 35% implied probability edging Communist Party (KPRF) at 32% and Liberal Democratic Party (LDPR) at 28.5%. WCIOM surveys show NL surging to 13-17%—its highest yet—bolstered by March leadership approvals positioning it as a fresh alternative amid LDPR's post-Zhirinovsky stagnation and KPRF's stable but aging base around 11%. FOM polls, however, favor LDPR at 13% over NL's 8%, highlighting pollster discrepancies and administrative resource gaps in Russia's mixed proportional-single member district system. Separation could arise from upcoming party primaries, candidate lists, or economic pressures shifting protest votes before the September 18-20 election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent U.S. Senator Ashley B. Moody commands 94.5% trader consensus as the Republican primary frontrunner for Florida's special Senate election, driven by her seamless qualification during the April 20-24 window and the absence of credible challengers among fringe entrants like A.C. Toulme, Michaelangelo Hamilton, and Jake Lang. Her prior role as Florida Attorney General, gubernatorial appointment to the vacancy in January 2025, and strong general election polling—leading Democrat Alexander Vindman by 7-10 points in recent Echelon Insights and Stetson surveys—bolster GOP unity ahead of the August 18 primary. Upsets remain possible via a late-breaking scandal, high-profile endorsement shift, or legal hurdles, though structural incumbency advantages and weak opposition make these low-probability risks.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a low likelihood of US territorial acquisition of any part of Greenland at 85.5% for No, driven by Denmark's and Greenland's firm rejections of sovereignty transfer despite President Trump's repeated calls for purchase or control on national security grounds amid Arctic competition with Russia and China. Recent April 2026 developments, including US-Danish talks for expanded military base access like additional sites near Thule Air Base, emphasize leasing arrangements under the 1951 Defense Agreement rather than annexation, reinforcing Danish constitutional barriers and public opposition. With no verified progress toward land sovereignty by year-end and NATO alliance strains, traders view outright acquisition as improbable barring unprecedented diplomatic breakthroughs.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Argentina's 2026 annual inflation in a tight race between the 25-29.9% (32%) and 30-34.9% (31.5%) bins, reflecting March 2026 CPI data showing 3.4% monthly inflation—the highest year-to-date—pushing year-over-year rates to 32.6% amid rising transportation and education costs. President Milei's fiscal austerity has slowed annual inflation from 2025 peaks above 200%, but reacceleration risks from seasonal factors and looser monetary signals have lifted central bank surveys to 29.1% full-year expectations and IMF forecasts to 30.5%. Key differentiators include April CPI (due mid-May) and sustained monthly deceleration below 3%; persistent 3%+ prints favor higher bins, while sub-2.5% trajectory supports the lower range.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending MLS Cup champions Inter Miami CF hold trader consensus at 17.5% implied probability to repeat, driven by Lionel Messi's star power and playoff pedigree despite sitting third in the Eastern Conference with 19 points from 11 games (5W-4D-2L, +3 GD) after a humiliating Matchday 11 defeat. LAFC (11.5%) and Vancouver Whitecaps (10.1%) trail closely, buoyed by solid Western starts—Vancouver's explosive 25 points (8W-1D-1L, +20 GD) as runners-up and LAFC's 21 points (6W-3D-2L, +11 GD)—but recent draws like LAFC's 2-2 vs. San Diego FC underscore volatility. Nashville SC (23 points atop East) and San Jose Earthquakes (28 points leading West at 9W-1D-1L) fuel the tight race, highlighting MLS parity just 10-11 games into the 34-match regular season before single-elimination playoffs.

markets.question: Will Club Atlético de Madrid win on 2026-05-12?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 12, 2026
If Club Atlético de Madrid wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CA Osasuna vs. Club Atlético de Madrid
events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for Tuesday, May 12, 2026 between CA Osasuna and Club Atlético de Madrid.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices this La Liga clash as a virtual coin flip, with Osasuna's fortress-like home record at El Sadar—unbeaten in recent outings—offsetting Atlético Madrid's higher table position and attacking prowess despite mounting defensive injuries. Osasuna gained momentum from a gritty 2-1 victory over Sevilla on April 26, bolstering their mid-table standing, while Atlético staged a 3-2 comeback win against Athletic Club on April 25 but will miss key midfielder Pablo Barrios (thigh) and defender José Giménez (muscle) through early May. Head-to-head history shows tight contests, often low-scoring draws, with both sides' recent form underscoring the upset potential and stalemate risk ahead of confirmed starting XIs.

events.title: Konyaspor vs. Fenerbahçe SK
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Süper Lig game, scheduled for Saturday, May 9, 2026 between Konyaspor and Fenerbahçe SK.

eventMetadata.context_description: Konyaspor's shocking 1-0 extra-time victory over Fenerbahçe in the Turkish Cup quarterfinal on April 21 at home has dramatically leveled trader sentiment for this Super Lig clash, exposing defensive lapses in the visitors despite their second-place table position behind Galatasaray. Hosting at Medaş Konya Büyükşehir Stadyumu, Konyaspor leverages strong home form against top sides, where they've secured draws and upsets, while Fenerbahçe's historical head-to-head dominance (18 wins to 7) is tempered by recent vulnerabilities amid title pressure and end-of-season fatigue. Minor injuries like Konyaspor's Yhoan Andzouana (muscle) sideline key depth, but full-strength lineups featuring Fener's Ederson, Skriniar, and Kanté keep probabilities tightly bunched around 50%, with draw consensus reflecting a gritty, low-scoring stalemate potential.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices PSOE-A at 30-32 seats (41%) as the leading outcome for the May 17 Andalusian regional election to the 109-seat Parliament, reflecting the upper range of recent April polls projecting 26-34 seats (averaging 27-28 from CIS, Sigma Dos, GAD3, and others). This positioning stems from PSOE-A's modest recovery from 2025 lows under leader María Jesús Montero, bolstered by fallout from the PP-led government's October 2025 breast cancer screening scandal—exposing 2,000+ delayed diagnoses, record manipulation, and Health Minister resignation—which has eroded the incumbent's "good manager" image without collapsing their projected 53-58 seats. Vox's 13-20 seat gains and left fragmentation (Por Andalucía, Adelante Andalucía) constrain PSOE upside, while 24-26 seats (29.5%) captures downside risk in this former socialist stronghold amid national PSOE headwinds. Final campaign pushes and turnout could tip ranges before resolution.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Patrick Mahomes leads at 61% implied probability as the Chiefs' Week 1 starting QB for 2026, fueled by recent ACL rehab updates showing him ahead of schedule—throwing passes and on track for OTAs after December 2025 surgery—bolstering trader confidence in his timely return despite typical nine-month timelines. Justin Fields sits at 25.5% following his March trade from the Jets as primary insurance, with Andy Reid's April endorsement calling him a "legitimate starting NFL QB" ready for early reps if needed. Chris Oladokun's 24.9% reflects his entrenched practice squad role and offseason buzz as a surprising contender over Fields, per mid-April reports, while veterans Joe Flacco (18.8%) and Gardner Minshew (8.1%) linger as free-agent fallback options amid roster depth including draft pick Garrett Nussmeier.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Trump administration's abrupt Pentagon announcement to withdraw about 5,000 US troops from Germany within six to 12 months—made without prior coordination with NATO allies—has sparked alarm and speculation over alliance cohesion amid transatlantic feuds on Middle East policy, burden-sharing, and sanctions against Spain and tensions with Italy. This repositioning signals pressure for higher European defense spending but stops well short of formal NATO withdrawal, which requires one year's notice under Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty and faces steep congressional hurdles given bipartisan support and historical reluctance despite past threats. Traders price low odds on near-term exit due to these legal and political barriers; NATO's ongoing consultations and potential summit outcomes could sway sentiment.

eventMetadata.context_description: With President Emmanuel Macron term-limited after two consecutive terms, France's 2027 presidential election—scheduled for April with a possible second-round runoff two weeks later—features a crowded, fragmented field where ballot access requires 500 signatures from elected officials like mayors. Hard-left La France Insoumise leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon's May 4 announcement of a fourth presidential bid has sharpened left-wing divisions, despite his past pledge to step aside, amid efforts to unify post-2024 hung parliament. Far-right Rassemblement National's Jordan Bardella leads first-round polls following March municipal election gains, while centrists Édouard Philippe and Gabriel Attal top hypothetical runoffs against extremes. Conservatives like Bruno Retailleau declared in February; party primaries and signature drives will clarify the ballot through early 2027.

markets.question: Clavicular sentenced to prison?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Braden Eric Peters (known as Clavicular) is sentenced to any time in jail/prison for any charges, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will remain open through December 31, 2026 or until Clavicular is sentenced to time in jail/prison, regardless of any initial sentencing that does not include prison. Any sentencing will qualify, regardless of subsequent appeals or changes to the sentence.

Civil lawsuits, regulatory actions, administrative complaints, investigations without charges, internal employment actions, or public accusations without the filing of criminal charges will not qualify, even if widely reported or described colloquially as “charges.”

If at any point all charges against Clavicular for all criminal cases are dropped for whatever reason, due to a plea agreement without an admission of guilt, a dismissal, or any other situation in which no judgement is rendered or will be rendered by the court, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government and law enforcement; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Shake Shack (SHAK) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Shake Shack is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Shake Shack’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.12 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Shake Shack reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.12 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Shake Shack releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final: Philippines vs Indonesia - Toss Match Double Philippines Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Philippines and Indonesia scheduled for 2026-05-07 in T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Philippines will be considered correct if  Philippines is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Indonesia will be considered correct if Indonesia is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

Philippines enter with strong recent form, winning four of their last five T20Is against Indonesia—including a dominant 78-run victory on December 11, 2025—and boasting a W L W W W W streak, while Indonesia slumped to a loss against Malaysia on May 1 amid an L L W L L L run. Absent pitch reports or weather updates, teams lack clear preference for batting or bowling first, keeping the pre-match ritual tightly contested.

markets.question: Will there be exactly 5 earthquakes of magnitude 6.5 or higher worldwide from May 4 - 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 6.5 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between May 4, 2026, 12:00 AM ET, and May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

This market may not resolve until the timeframe of this market has concluded. If a qualifying earthquake has been recorded on the final day, this market may remain open for 24 hours to allow for revisions to the recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a recent lull in global seismicity, with no magnitude 6.5 or greater earthquakes recorded by USGS since the M7.4 off Japan on April 20—over two weeks ago—positioning zero events at 54% implied probability for May 4-10. This quiet period follows two M7.4s in early April but aligns with USGS long-term averages of 15-20 such events annually worldwide (roughly 0.3-0.4 per week), where Poisson variability often yields null weeks. No seismic swarms, foreshock sequences, or strain accumulation on major subduction zones or transform faults currently signal elevated risk. A M6.0 struck the Philippines on May 4, but fell short of the moment magnitude threshold; real-time USGS feeds through May 10 could shift odds with new detections.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump publicly insult someone on May 16, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump makes any public statement in which he insults, mocks, or attacks any non-fictional individual personally or professionally in a clearly negative manner on the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

This includes calling the individual weak, stupid, disloyal, a failure, using an insulting nickname, using other derogatory language, or using the negative form of a positive trait in a derogatory personal way (e.g., “He/She isn’t smart”). Negative forms used in reference to the individual's professional actions, policies, or decisions (e.g., “He/She isn’t being smart about this policy”) will not count. Policy disagreements stated without disparaging language will not count.

A direct reference will qualify even if the individual is not named, so long as it is reasonably clear from context that they are the subject.

Any written, verbal, or recorded public statement by Trump qualifies.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Recent catalysts include Rubio's May 5 White House briefing urging Beijing to pressure Iran over the Strait of Hormuz blockade—timed with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi's arrival in China—and warnings against Taiwan destabilization ahead of President Trump's May 14-15 Xi Jinping summit in Beijing, where Rubio may accompany as top diplomat. Elevated odds for a December 31 visit reflect necessities like trade tensions, secondary sanctions threats, and Hormuz sea lanes, outweighing Rubio's hawkish record; a June 30 outcome remains competitive if he joins Trump's trip, though no confirmation exists.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

South America's 20.5% reflects elite contenders Argentina (defending champions), Brazil, Uruguay, Colombia, Ecuador, and Paraguay, whose Copa América pedigree and CONMEBOL qualifier dominance keep them viable despite fewer spots. Lower odds for Africa (CAF's nine slots including Senegal, Egypt), Asia (AFC's eight like Japan, South Korea), North America (hosts plus CONCACAF qualifiers), and Oceania stem from historical knockout struggles and tougher paths in the expanded 48-team group stage and playoffs, with March 2026 UEFA playoffs and the final draw solidifying Europe's edge through favorable matchups and form.

markets.question: Map Handicap: MOUZ (-1.5) vs Gentle Mates (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between MOUZ and Gentle Mates in the PGL Astana Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ" if MOUZ wins 2 or more maps than Gentle Mates in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Gentle Mates". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: MOUZ vs Gentle Mates (BO3) - PGL Astana Group Stage
events.description:

This market will resolve to "MOUZ" if MOUZ win the match against Gentle Mates.

This market will resolve to "Gentle Mates" if Gentle Mates win the match against MOUZ.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: MOUZ enters as the clear trader consensus favorite against Gentle Mates in PGL Astana 2026 Swiss group stage round 1 BO3, backed by their #9 world ranking, perfect 2-0 head-to-head record including a dominant ESL Pro League S22 win, and consistent top-10 finishes like 3rd at PGL Cluj-Napoca. Recent roster shakeup benched Jimpphat and Brollan for newcomers jL and xelex just 17 days ago, contributing to mixed form with losses to Spirit and FURIA offset by wins over Aurora and Legacy. Lower-ranked #35 Gentle Mates shows qualifier momentum, winning three of five recent BO3s against mid-tier foes like FOKUS and 9INE, but struggled versus G2 and RED Canids; map edges favor them on Inferno (82% win rate) while MOUZ excels on Mirage (73%) and Overpass (100%), setting up key veto battles in this high-stakes opener where a win advances 1-0 in Swiss.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

markets.question: Trump-Denmark Greenland deal signed by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if both Denmark and the United States sign a deal, treaty, or similar international agreement of any kind relating to Greenland by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any U.S.–Danish agreement relating to Greenland will qualify, regardless of subject matter, including but not limited to sovereignty, governance, security arrangements, or access to natural resources.

Examples of qualifying deals include but are not limited to a treaty that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession (even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement treaty establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission; or agreements permitting additional U.S. troop stationing, basing access, or resource extraction rights in Greenland.

This market will resolve to “Yes” only if a qualifying agreement is formally signed by authorized representatives of both Denmark and the United States. Official announcements, statements of intent, or declarations that an agreement has been reached will not suffice unless accompanied by signatures from both sides. Whether or not a qualifying deal is later passed by the respective parliaments or enters into force will not affect this market’s resolution. Signaling from Greenland’s population will not be considered.

Announcements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or understandings that are not formally signed by both parties will not qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction, control, basing rights, or access arrangements in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying agreements.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 91.5% implied probability against a Trump-Denmark Greenland deal signed by June 30, driven by Denmark's and Greenland's unwavering insistence that sovereignty remains non-negotiable. Trump's January 2026 revival of acquisition ambitions—initially backed by tariff threats on NATO allies and military rhetoric—prompted massive protests in Nuuk, European solidarity with Copenhagen, and early Danish elections in March amid public backlash. While Davos talks led to plans updating the 1951 defense agreement for expanded US military access and barring Chinese/Russian minerals exploitation, no sovereignty-transferring pact has materialized. Recent US military expansion bids in April face ongoing resistance, leaving scant time for formal signing amid diplomatic entanglements.

Trader consensus prices in frontrunners' momentum, head-to-head edges, and roster stability amid patch meta shifts, with upsets possible in remaining matches.

eventMetadata.context_description: Borussia Dortmund, sitting second in the Bundesliga standings behind Bayern Munich, host mid-table Eintracht Frankfurt on May 8 at Signal Iduna Park amid a tight title race with 12 games remaining. Dortmund's recent form shows vulnerability after losses to Bayer Leverkusen (0-1 on April 11) and Hoffenheim (1-2 on April 18), but they secured a 2-0 win over VfB Stuttgart earlier in April; key striker Serhou Guirassy has returned to training ahead of tomorrow's Freiburg clash, boosting attack despite absences like Niklas Süle and Karim Adeyemi. Frankfurt, eighth with mixed results including a 1-3 home defeat to RB Leipzig last weekend and a 2-1 win at Wolfsburg, face defensive woes from injuries to Nnamdi Collins (season-ending), Rasmus Kristensen (ankle), and Ansgar Knauff. Dortmund hold a strong head-to-head edge, winning recent encounters, with home advantage and Frankfurt's injury crisis shaping trader consensus toward the hosts in this pivotal late-season matchup.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States' long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point by December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
events.description:

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 74.5% implied probability against another major US sovereign credit rating downgrade before 2027, reflecting Fitch Ratings' April 30, 2026 affirmation of its AA+ stable outlook despite projecting US debt-to-GDP at 122% by year-end—up from 116.6% in 2025—amid widening fiscal deficits averaging $1.9 trillion annually per CBO estimates. This stability follows Moody's Aa1 downgrade in May 2025 and prior S&P/Fitch actions, with agencies citing chronic governance gridlock but no acute triggers like debt ceiling default risks, as the $41.1 trillion limit provides headroom above current $39 trillion levels. Key catalysts include FY2027 budget talks and potential debt limit hikes, where bipartisan resolutions have historically averted crises, underscoring the resilience of US Treasuries backed by global reserve demand.

markets.question: Will Khamenei post 25-29 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seattle be between 64-65°F on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service guidance and ensemble model consensus from GFS and ECMWF project Seattle's high temperature on May 7 clustering in the mid-60s°F, driving trader sentiment toward 64-65°F (36.5% implied probability) and 66-67°F (35.5%) as leading outcomes. This tight race reflects uncertainty in persistent marine stratus clouds from Pacific onshore flow, which could suppress peaks by 1-2°F if slow to clear versus faster dissipation allowing greater solar heating. Historical May 7 averages hover near 64°F at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (KSEA), the likely resolution station, with lower probabilities for extremes aligning with climatological baselines. Key differentiators include boundary layer mixing and afternoon wind shifts; monitor NWS Seattle's next forecast discussion for updates from observational data.

markets.question: Will Vivek Ramaswamy win the Ohio Governor Republican Primary Election by between 50% and 60%?
markets.description:
The Republican Primary for Governor of Ohio is currently scheduled to take place on May 5, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including those from the Ohio Republican Party.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Vivek Ramaswamy capturing 60-70% of the vote in Ohio's Republican gubernatorial primary, reflecting pre-election polls like the April BGSU/YouGov survey showing him at 76% against challengers Casey Putsch and Heather Hill at 12% each, amplified by endorsements from President Trump, Gov. Mike DeWine, and the Ohio GOP that cleared much of the primary field. Yesterday's May 5 primary confirmed this dominance with early returns at 98% precincts reported giving Ramaswamy 82.5% statewide (673,902 votes) to Putsch's 17.5% (143,257), yet the skin-in-the-game crowd prices the final certified tally—potentially adjusted by remaining absentees and provisionals—landing in that bin. Barring an improbable surge in late Putsch ballots from urban or disputed precincts, this positioning holds firm ahead of official certification.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Open Market Committee held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% following its April 28-29, 2026 meeting, as elevated inflation—headline CPI at 3.3% year-over-year in March, core at 2.6%—offset resilient labor markets with unemployment dipping to 4.3%. The March dot plot projects a median rate of around 3.00%-3.25% by end-2027, signaling gradual easing contingent on disinflation progress. CME FedWatch Tool shows 95% odds of no change at the June 16-17 meeting, aligning trader consensus with 10-year Treasury yields near 4.4%. Key catalysts include May CPI data and the June FOMC, which could shift market-implied rate paths amid sticky price pressures.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

events.title: Which parties will be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful vote of confidence in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections, no party secured a majority in the 90-seat National Assembly, with Robert Golob's centre-left Freedom Movement (GS) taking 29 seats and Janez Janša's right-wing Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) 28. After Golob's broad coalition bid collapsed in late April—rejecting SDS involvement—and President Nataša Pirc Musar declined to mandate a prime minister-designate due to insufficient support (46 votes needed), centre-right talks led by Janša advanced rapidly. On May 5, Janša announced agreement on coalition guidelines with the NSi-SLS-FOKUS bloc (9 seats), eyeing Democrats (DEM, 6 seats) and external Resni.ca (5 seats) backing for a potential minority government. Traders watch for a parliamentary vote or snap election risk amid stalled negotiations.

markets.question: Will T1 make a roster change before July?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves T1's active League of Legends roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the T1 Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/T1) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Liquipedia; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no criminal charges against Les Wexner by June 30, reflecting the absence of any DOJ indictment despite heightened scrutiny from recently released Epstein files. In February 2026, a House Oversight Committee deposition saw Wexner, Epstein's longtime financial patron, deny knowledge of the sex offender's crimes, claiming he was duped; lawmakers questioned his credibility but took no further prosecutorial steps. A civil lawsuit filed by Epstein survivors in early April alleges Wexner enabled the abuse through funding and property, yet distinguishes from criminal proceedings. With no active federal investigation or scheduled hearings announced, and Wexner uncharged since Epstein's 2019 death, traders price significant barriers to near-term prosecution.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors no Trump-Putin meeting by June 30, reflecting the absence of any announced in-person summit amid ongoing bilateral phone diplomacy. Their latest call on April 29—the 12th since early 2025—centered on Ukraine ceasefire proposals and brief truces, yet produced no travel or venue commitments, echoing earlier Kremlin denials of immediate plans like a China sideline. Reports of a U.S. invitation for Putin to the December G20 in Miami further point beyond the deadline. While NATO tensions and troop shifts add context, no diplomatic breakthroughs signal an imminent face-to-face; sudden escalations in Ukraine talks or third-country mediation could shift odds.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $2.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=NGD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Henry Hub natural gas spot prices have stabilized near $2.70 per million British thermal units (MMBtu) amid shoulder-season dynamics, with the latest EIA Weekly Natural Gas Storage Report showing inventories at 2,142 billion cubic feet (Bcf) as of April 24, 2026—up 79 Bcf week-over-week and well above the five-year average. Robust U.S. dry gas production near record highs, coupled with moderate LNG export flows, has capped upside, driving recent futures to post-winter lows around $2.48/MMBtu. Mild spring weather has curbed heating and early cooling demand, fostering trader sentiment for range-bound trading through May. Key catalysts include weekly EIA storage releases starting May 7 and emerging summer heat forecasts influencing power sector consumption.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in London be 13°C on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Met Office forecasts position London's highest temperature on May 7 at 16°C under variable cloud with sunny spells and a 40-70% shower risk in afternoon hours, driving trader consensus toward 15°C (49%) and 16°C (36.5%) as leading outcomes. Nearby low pressure limits solar insolation and caps daytime heating despite a mild southerly airflow preventing colder readings below 14°C, aligning with ECMWF ensemble means and historical May 7 averages near Heathrow of 16°C. Light winds (gusts to 16 mph) and patchy precipitation introduce uncertainty, with cloud breaks during 2-4 PM peak heating as a key variable; final hourly guidance expected overnight from the Met Office could refine market-implied odds before official observations resolve the market.

markets.question: Will Rare Earths Americas not IPO before July 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Rare Earths Americas' market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

As of market creation, the IPO is scheduled to price on May 6 (ET). If no such IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before July 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency.

It is calculated as the total number of outstanding shares, multiplied by the official closing share price of the publicly traded class on the first trading day.

If necessary, to accurately capture the company’s total market capitalization, rather than a stock-class-specific market capitalization, the calculation will include all outstanding share classes and apply any stated conversion ratios to the publicly traded class. Where no conversion right exists, such shares will be counted at their stated outstanding amount without discount, unless official filings explicitly specify differently.

The number of outstanding shares will be determined from official company filings or disclosures (e.g., SEC filings). The closing share price on the first trading day will be determined from the primary exchange’s official listing page.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official company filings and the primary exchange’s official listing page. The market capitalization will be determined through appropriate calculation using the total outstanding shares and the closing price from the first day of trading.

In the event of an interruption in the normal trading session on the specified company’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that day as the first day of trading for the purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rare Earths Americas, an exploration-stage critical minerals company, launched its IPO roadshow on April 28, 2026, targeting a $368 million fully diluted valuation by offering 2.78 million shares at $17–$19 each on NYSE American under ticker REA, aiming to raise up to $52.8 million. Trader sentiment reflects enthusiasm for its high-grade heavy rare earth projects in the US (Shiloh, Alpha) and Brazil (Constellation), fueled by US policy mandates for non-Chinese supply chains amid defense and EV demand, alongside recent sector consolidation like USA Rare Earth's $2.8 billion Serra Verde acquisition. However, pre-revenue status and commodity price volatility temper expectations, with pricing and debut likely imminent, hinging on roadshow feedback and mining sector trends.

markets.question: NHL Playoffs: Who Will Win Series? - Ducks vs. Golden Knights
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Ducks” if the Anaheim Ducks win the 2026 NHL Playoffs Second Round series between the Anaheim Ducks and Vegas Golden Knights. This market will resolve to “Knights” if the Vegas Golden Knights win the 2026 NHL Playoffs Second Round series between the Anaheim Ducks and Vegas Golden Knights.

If a partial series is played and not completed by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the 2026 NHL Playoffs are cancelled, postponed after May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NHL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights hold a 1-0 series lead over the Anaheim Ducks in the Western Conference Second Round of the Stanley Cup Playoffs after a 3-1 Game 1 victory at home, where they capitalized despite being outshot 34-22 and controlling less 5-on-5 play. William Karlsson's return from a six-month lower-body injury bolstered Vegas' center depth, contributing to their defensive structure and timely scoring from Ivan Barbashev. The Ducks, in their first playoff series since 2018 after upsetting a higher seed in Round 1, showed resilience with strong forechecking but struggled to beat Vegas goaltender Adin Hill. Game 2 remains in Las Vegas, amplifying the Knights' home-ice advantage and playoff experience as traders price them at 77.5% to win the series, reflecting superior roster depth and recent momentum despite Anaheim's underdog fightback potential.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol remains in custody following his February 2026 conviction and life sentence for insurrection tied to the 2024 martial law declaration, with an appeals court on April 29 increasing his term to seven years for obstruction of arrest. These severe penalties, upheld amid multiple detention warrant extensions, reflect judicial consensus on charges from the political crisis that led to his impeachment and ouster by the Constitutional Court in April 2025. Absent successful higher appeals or extraordinary pardons—unlikely given the timeline to December 31, 2026—traders price a 92.5% implied probability of "No," emphasizing entrenched legal barriers over release scenarios.

markets.question: Spread: Gwangju FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming K-League game, scheduled for May 9 at 3:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Gwangju FC" if Gwangju FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Gangwon FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on kleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Gwangju FC vs. Gangwon FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the K-League game, scheduled for May 9 at 3:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gangwon's recent dominance over Gwangju FC, including a 3-0 victory in their earlier K League 1 matchup this season and an unbeaten streak across the last six head-to-heads, underpins trader consensus favoring the visitors despite Gwangju's home advantage at Gwangju Football Stadium. Gwangju languish in 12th place after 10 matches with just six points and a league-worst -16 goal difference, mired in a five-game losing skid marked by defensive frailties (23 goals conceded). Gangwon sit comfortably 6th on 13 points, buoyed by solid recent form (wins in three of last five) and tactical adjustments fueling their mid-table push, though Gwangju's desperation for points could spark an upset in this relegation scrap.

markets.question: Will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit (HIGH) $160 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Airbnb (ABNB) shares closed at $138.86 on May 5, reflecting a modest 2.31% year-to-date gain amid building anticipation for Q1 2026 earnings on May 7, where Wall Street anticipates $0.30 EPS—a 25% year-over-year increase—and $2.62 billion in revenue, up 15%. Recent momentum stems from Oppenheimer's upgrade to Outperform with a $180 price target, citing materializing organic revenue drivers like travel services scaling and prospective 2026 World Cup tailwinds, against a consensus analyst target of $153. Q4 2025 delivered revenue beats despite EPS misses, with full-year guidance signaling low-teens growth acceleration. Traders eye nights-and-seats booked and EBITDA margins for directional cues, as earnings reaction could spur May volatility around key price thresholds.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Joe Wilson anchors trader consensus at 83% implied probability for a Republican Party hold in solidly red SC-02 (R+7 PVI, Trump +14 in 2024), bolstered by his $294,000 cash-on-hand vastly outpacing GOP primary challengers Sam Gibbons and Hamp Redmond ahead of the June 9 primaries. Wilson's history of 55-60% general election margins against weak Democratic opposition sustains low 18% odds for Democrats, whose primary features four underfunded contenders—Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, David Robinson II, and Daniel Shrief—plus independent Dayna Alane Smith (Workers Party). Candidate-specific outcomes A, B, and Other trade near 50¢ on low volume amid pre-primary uncertainty for the November 4 general election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Roberto Bautista Agut, the 38-year-old Spaniard ranked No. 89 and retiring at season's end, faces Italian wild card Francesco Maestrelli (No. 112) in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia first-round clash on Rome's outdoor clay. Bautista Agut enters off mixed clay results—retiring early versus Berrettini in Monte Carlo, a straight-sets loss to Tirante in Madrid, but a Challenger win over Gueymard Wayenburg before falling to Tabilo in Aix-en-Provence last week—showcasing grit amid form dips. Maestrelli, buoyed by home-crowd support at Foro Italico, arrives winless in recent clay outings, including qualifying defeats in Madrid and Monte Carlo plus early Challenger exits. With no head-to-head and Bautista Agut's superior Masters 1000 experience on the surface, traders weigh veteran pedigree against the underdog's motivation and upset potential in this bottom-half matchup.

markets.question: Will Likud win 25-29 seats in the 2026 Israeli legislative election?
markets.description:
Legislative elections are expected to be held in Israel on October 27, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the number of seats won by the Likud party in the Israeli Knesset as a result of this election.

If the Likud party contests this election as part of a joint candidate list with one or more parties, all seats won by the joint candidate list will be counted. If the Likud party merges with another party prior to the election, all seats won by the resultant merged party, or any joint candidate list through which it contests the election, will be counted.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by October 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the Likud party in this election.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Israel’s Central Election Committee (Va'adet HaBehirot HaMerkazit) (https://www.gov.il/en/departments/central-elections-committee/govil-landing-page).
